executive_summary: Business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 crisis. Net margin £1,330,126 (7.0% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark). Hedging cost £4,044,933 vs going naked (85% mandate price). 445 bill shock events (107 in crisis years); 17/18 retention offers accepted.

insights.headline: Hedging cost £4,044,933 vs going naked (85% mandate price)
insights.narrative: Whole-run commodity basis: actual net £1,330,126 vs naked £5,375,059. The 85% minimum hedge mandate cost £4,044,933 over the 10-year window. Worst year: 2022 (hedging cost £988,133 vs naked). Hedging reduces variance (survival objective) at the cost of expected P&L.

insights.headline: 445 bill shock events (107 in crisis years); 17/18 retention offers accepted
insights.narrative: Bill shocks across 2016-2025: 445 events, 8 extreme (>100%). 2021-22 accounted for 107 shocks as wholesale prices spiked. Retention: 17/18 offers accepted (94%); 5 customers churned without an offer (margin guard blocked). Enterprise value: £6,124,101 across 14 accounts.

insights.headline: Risk committee: 38 interventions; SURVIVED full 2016-2025 window
insights.narrative: The risk committee (Context Handshake) intervened 38 times over 10 years, with 9 in the 2021-22 crisis. Business survived: No administration event -- Ofgem solvency floor maintained.. The 85% minimum hedge mandate limited worst-case exposure.

insights.headline: Net margin £1,330,126 (7.0% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark)
insights.narrative: Gross margin £6,559,771, net £1,330,126 (7.0% of revenue). Industry benchmark: 2-5% -- this business sits above that range. I&C customers contribute 99% of net margin (£1,322,711); residential 1% (£7,415). WARNING: I&C accounts for 99% of margin -- high concentration risk. Cost to serve: £91,653; net after CTS: £6,454,351.

insights.headline: 1531 bills issued; service quality 0.911/1.0, clarity 0.846/1.0
insights.narrative: 1531 bills issued over 2016-2025 (avg 153/yr). Service quality: 0.911 (excellent). Bill clarity: 0.846 (good). Cost to serve: £91,653 portfolio total, £6,547/account/run.

coherence_narrative: The dominant theme is crisis survival. The business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 energy crisis, with the 85% hedge mandate cost £4,044,933 vs going naked, 107 bill shocks in the 2021-22 crisis, and 17/18 retention offers accepted. net margin £1,330,126 (7.0% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark), with I&C customers contributing 99% of net margin. The hedging mandate limited worst-case downside at the cost of expected P&L in calmer years; the I&C book was the primary profit engine throughout. Without I&C revenue the residential book would be loss-making -- concentration risk is the key strategic vulnerability.
recommended_actions: I&C concentration critical (99% of margin): target acquisition of 2 additional I&C customers to reduce dependency
Hedge mandate cost £4,044,933 vs going naked: review minimum hedge fraction for non-crisis years (current 85% floor)
107 crisis-year bill shocks: consider tariff smoothing mechanism to spread 2021-22 wholesale spike across 2020-2023 for residential customers
